parsed_review.summary: CP00-428 is a descriptor-only RP14 (Partner Settlement) closeout slice covering 40 planned units (RP14.P02.M01.S17-RP14.P02.M03.S14) across M01 Contract Draft tail (4), M02 Type And Shape Definition (22), and M03 Primary Implementation Slice head (14). Independent read-only inspection of all eight in-scope refs (settlement-core-contract.json, README.md, src/index.js, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, test/model.test.js, validate-rp14-settlement-core-contract.mjs) confirms the pack-level claims are supported: every generator returns frozen descriptor objects with the full no-write/no-leak attestation (writes_product_state:false, no_real_data:true, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, exposes_permission_decision_detail:false, etc.); there is no settlement/origination/allocation/working-credit runtime, no DB/object-storage writes, no real or fixture client data, and no network/connector use. Deliverable and micro distribution match the baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and registry requirements exactly; the contract JSON is in sync with the generators (spot-checked M01/M02 rows are descriptor-only with only base properties), and historical_pack_bindings ends at CP00-428. Authority boundary is correctly enforced in code, contract, and manifest: claude_is_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/review-receipt descriptors, not product runtime. production_ready is honestly reported as false (implemented_pending_review / descriptor-verified), all 26 recorded validation commands exit 0, and the review-hardening preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output, or missing fields). Unrelated dirty/untracked files (including the ldip c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly excluded from active scope. One non-blocking P3 maintainability observation: the inherited ROW_EXTRAS spread is inert for CP428. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; the pack is sound for closeout, with final enterprise-trust approval remaining a human responsibility outside this read-only review.

findings.title: Inert ROW_EXTRAS inheritance in CP428 descriptor builder
findings.details: SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP428_ROW_EXTRAS (lines 355-357) is defined as { ...SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP427_ROW_EXTRAS }, and createSettlementCoreCp428P02ContractImplementationSliceCaseSet merges it per row via ...(SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP428_ROW_EXTRAS[key] ?? {}). However, none of CP428's row-key set (rollback_behavior, retry_behavior, unit_test_happy_path/denied_path/review_path, service_entrypoint_contract, request_normalization, tenant_boundary_precheck, matter_trace_precheck, permission_precheck, audit_hint_precheck, primary_happy_path, secondary_workflow_path, state_transition_enforcement, idempotency_key_handling, lock_acquisition_rule, persistence_boundary, validation_error_mapping, review_required_routing, approval_required_routing, blocked_claim_output, integration_smoke_case) matches any extras key (scope_inventory, permission_baseline_note, tenant_scope_field, state_transition_map, documentation_entry, etc.), which are all keyed to the earlier model-foundation row titles used by CP426/CP427. The inherited extras therefore contribute nothing to any CP428 row. This is confirmed empirically: the emitted rows in contracts/settlement-core-contract.json (e.g. lines 4583-4615 for M01, 4646-4670 for M02) carry only the five base fields (title, descriptor_only, runtime_execution, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included, section_micro_phase_id). The construct is harmless and has zero effect on descriptor output, boundary attestation, or validation, but it is dead/misleading for future maintainers who may assume the extras apply to CP428 rows.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup in a future RP14 pack: either drop the unused SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP428_ROW_EXTRAS spread (and the per-row merge) for CP428, or add a brief note that CP428 implementation-slice rows intentionally carry no row-specific extras. No change required for CP00-428 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking maintainability observation. Does not affect descriptor-only correctness, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, authority boundary, or coverage. Acknowledged and deferred to optional future cleanup; does not block CP00-428 pack or goal closeout.
